seoTitle: How to Import an n8n Template Into Your Workspace
metaDescription: Learn how to browse the n8n template library or community hub and import pre-built workflows into your workspace instantly.
intro: Rather than building common automations from scratch, you can browse n8n's built-in template library or the wider community hub and import a pre-built workflow in seconds. This guide covers both the in-app template browser and the copy-paste method for community-shared workflow JSON.

steps.heading: Open a fresh workflow canvas
steps.body: Create a new, empty workflow in your n8n workspace to import a template into.

steps.heading: Browse the built-in Templates tab
steps.body: Check the sidebar Templates tab for curated, ready-to-use automation recipes organized by category.

steps.heading: Or browse the community library
steps.body: Alternatively, visit the official online n8n workflow library to find a specific community-built blueprint.

steps.heading: Copy the workflow JSON
steps.body: Click Copy Workflow to save the complete blueprint's JSON data to your clipboard.

steps.heading: Paste it onto your canvas
steps.body: Return to your n8n canvas and paste the code to instantly render all the template's nodes.

steps.heading: Reconnect credentials and test
steps.body: Update authentication for any connected apps, then run the workflow to confirm everything maps correctly.

faq.question: Do imported templates come with working credentials?
faq.answer: No, you'll need to reconnect and authenticate each app node yourself after importing a template.

faq.question: Is there a difference between the built-in Templates tab and the community library?
faq.answer: The built-in tab shows curated, verified templates inside n8n itself, while the community library is a broader online hub of user-submitted workflows.

faq.question: Can I modify a template after importing it?
faq.answer: Yes, once imported the template becomes a regular editable workflow you can customize freely.

faq.question: How do I know if a template will work with my current n8n version?
faq.answer: Test it after importing — some nodes may need updating if the template was built for an older or newer version of n8n.
